- [Classes & Objects](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/11-classes-objects.html): Blueprints for Building Anything in Dart
- [Constructors & Named Parameters](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/12-constructors-named-parameters.html): Multiple Ways to Build Your Objects
- [Inheritance & Mixins](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/13-inheritance-mixins.html): Sharing and Combining Abilities Across Classes
- [Null Safety](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/14-null-safety.html): Eliminating the Billion-Dollar Mistake in Dart
- [Async, Futures & Streams](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/15-async-futures-streams.html): Handling Things That Take Time in Dart
- [Everything is a Widget](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/16-everything-is-a-widget.html): The Building Blocks of Every Flutter App
- [StatelessWidget](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/17-statelesswidget.html): Simple Widgets That Never Change Their Mind
- [StatefulWidget & setState](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/18-statefulwidget-setstate.html): Widgets That Remember and React
- [MaterialApp & Scaffold](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/19-materialapp-scaffold.html): The Foundation Every Flutter App Stands On
- [Text, Icons & Images](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/20-text-icons-images.html): The Visual Vocabulary of Flutter
- [Buttons & User Interactions](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/21-buttons-user-interactions.html): Making Your App Respond to Taps
- [Row & Column Layout](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/22-row-column-layout.html): Horizontal and Vertical Arrangement
- [Container, Padding & SizedBox](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/23-container-padding-sizedbox.html): Spacing, Sizing, and Decoration
- [ListView & GridView](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/24-listview-gridview.html): Scrollable Lists and Grids
- [Stack & Positioned](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/25-stack-positioned.html): Overlapping Widgets Like Layers
- [Forms & Text Input](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/26-forms-text-input.html): Collecting User Data with Validation
- [Building Custom Widgets](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/27-building-custom-widgets.html): Extract, Compose, and Reuse
- [Design System & Theming](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/28-design-system-theming.html): Consistent Look with ThemeData and Custom Design Systems
- [Navigation Basics](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/29-navigation-basics.html): Moving Between Screens with Navigator
- [GoRouter](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/30-gorouter.html): Declarative Routing with Path Parameters and Redirects
- [Passing Data Between Screens](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/31-passing-data-between-screens.html): Sending luggage on your route trips
- [Understanding State](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/32-understanding-state.html): The memory that makes your app alive
- [BLoC Pattern - Events & States](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/33-bloc-pattern-events-states.html): The traffic controller for your app's brain
- [flutter_bloc in Practice](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/34-flutter_bloc-in-practice.html): Wiring the BLoC brain to the Flutter body
- [Advanced BLoC Patterns](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/35-advanced-bloc-patterns.html): Battle-tested patterns from the production trenches
- [Clean Architecture Overview](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/36-clean-architecture-overview.html): Building a skyscraper with a solid blueprint
- [Domain Layer](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/37-domain-layer.html): Entities, Use Cases & the Repository Pattern
- [Data Layer](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/38-data-layer.html): The bridge between your app and the outside world
- [Presentation Layer](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/39-presentation-layer.html): Where your app meets the user's eyes
- [GetIt Service Locator](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/40-getit-service-locator.html): The universal toolbox for your app's dependencies
- [Injectable & Code Generation](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/41-injectable-code-generation.html): Auto-Wiring Your Dependencies
- [REST APIs & CRUD](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/42-rest-apis-crud.html): Create, Read, Update & Delete with HTTP
- [Dio HTTP Client](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/43-dio-http-client.html): Professional Networking for Flutter
- [JSON Models & Serialization](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/44-json-models-serialization.html): Turning JSON into Dart Objects
- [Hive Local Database](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/45-hive-local-database.html): Local CRUD — Create, Read, Update & Delete on Device
- [Token & Auth Storage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/46-token-auth-storage.html): Keeping Users Logged In Securely
- [Firebase Setup & Auth](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/47-firebase-setup-auth.html): Adding Firebase to Your Flutter App
- [Social Login](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/48-social-login.html): Sign In with Google & Apple
- [Error Handling & Result Pattern](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/49-error-handling-result-pattern.html): Failing Gracefully Like a Pro
- [Testing & Debugging](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/50-testing-debugging.html): Proving Your Code Works
- [Config, Build & Release](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-dart-masterclass/51-config-build-release.html): Shipping Your App to the World

## Flutter Interview Mastery: Zero to Senior in 77 Lessons

- Course page: https://learn.teamzlab.com/c/flutter-interview-mastery.html
- Level: Mid-Level to Senior
- Lesson count: 77
- About: Free interactive Flutter interview preparation course with 77 lessons. Master Dart, BLoC, Clean Architecture, testing, platform channels, CI/CD, system design, DSA, and behavioral interviews. Built…

### Lessons

- [Dart Types, Null Safety & Variables](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/01-dart-types-null-safety-variables.html): The Foundation Every Interviewer Tests First
- [Collections, Iterables & Equality](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/02-collections-iterables-equality.html): Data Structures That Show Up in Every Interview
- [Futures, async/await & the Event Loop](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/03-futures-asyncawait-the-event-loop.html): The #1 Most-Asked Dart Interview Topic
- [Streams, StreamController & Operators](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/04-streams-streamcontroller-operators.html): Real-time Data Flows — From Chat to Sensors
- [Isolates, compute() & Concurrency](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/05-isolates-compute-concurrency.html): When Single-Threaded Isn't Enough
- [Generics, Extensions, Mixins & Typedefs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/06-generics-extensions-mixins-typedefs.html): Advanced Dart That Separates Mid from Senior
- [Records, Patterns, Sealed Classes & Class Modifiers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/07-records-patterns-sealed-classes-class-modifiers.html): Modern Dart 3.x — The Features Interviewers Love to Ask
- [Error Handling, Result Types & Defensive Coding](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/08-error-handling-result-types-defensive-coding.html): Production Apps Crash Less When You Handle Errors Right
- [OOP, SOLID Principles & Composition](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/09-oop-solid-principles-composition.html): Architecture Interview Questions Start Here
- [Widget Tree, Element Tree & Render Tree](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/10-widget-tree-element-tree-render-tree.html): The Three Trees That Power Every Flutter Frame
- [Stateless vs Stateful Widget & Lifecycle](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/11-stateless-vs-stateful-widget-lifecycle.html): The Question Every Flutter Interview Starts With
- [BuildContext, InheritedWidget, Keys & Rebuild Boundaries](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/12-buildcontext-inheritedwidget-keys-rebuild-boundaries.html): The Internal Plumbing Every Senior Must Explain
- [Layout System, Constraints & Flex](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/13-layout-system-constraints-flex.html): Constraints Go Down, Sizes Go Up — Master This or Fight Flutter Forever
- [Navigation: Navigator vs GoRouter, Deep Links & Route Guards](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/14-navigation-navigator-vs-gorouter-deep-links-route-guards.html): Every Senior Flutter Dev Gets Asked This — Answer With Confidence
- [Forms, Validation, Focus & Controllers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/15-forms-validation-focus-controllers.html): Input Flows That Don't Frustrate Users — Senior-Level UX Patterns
- [State Management Landscape](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/16-state-management-landscape.html): How to Answer 'How Do You Choose State Management?' in an Interview
- [Cubit Fundamentals](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/17-cubit-fundamentals.html): The Simplest Step Up From setState — Master This Pattern First
- [BLoC Fundamentals](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/18-bloc-fundamentals.html): Events In, States Out — The Pattern That Scales to Any Team
- [Advanced BLoC Patterns](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/19-advanced-bloc-patterns.html): Patterns That Separate Junior From Senior BLoC Usage
- [Clean Architecture Fundamentals for Flutter](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/20-clean-architecture-fundamentals-for-flutter.html): The Answer That Gets You Hired at Senior Level
- [Repository Pattern, Use Cases, DI & Boundaries](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/21-repository-pattern-use-cases-di-boundaries.html): The Architecture Layer Every Senior Flutter Dev Must Master
- [MVVM vs MVI vs Clean vs MVC — Tradeoffs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/22-mvvm-vs-mvi-vs-clean-vs-mvc-tradeoffs.html): How to Answer 'Why Did You Choose This Architecture?' Without Being Dogmatic
- [REST APIs with Dio, Interceptors, Token Refresh & Retries](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/23-rest-apis-with-dio-interceptors-token-refresh-retries.html): Production HTTP Client Patterns Every Senior Flutter Dev Uses Daily
- [WebSockets, Socket.IO & Real-time Sync](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/24-websockets-socketio-real-time-sync.html): Building Live Collaboration Features That Actually Work at Scale
- [Local Persistence: SharedPreferences vs Hive vs SQLite vs Floor](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/25-local-persistence-sharedpreferences-vs-hive-vs-sqlite-vs.html): Choosing the Right Storage for the Right Job
- [Offline-First Architecture & Sync](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/26-offline-first-architecture-sync.html): The Most Differentiating Skill for Senior Flutter Engineers
- [Firebase Essentials: Auth, FCM, Crashlytics, Firestore](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/27-firebase-essentials-auth-fcm-crashlytics-firestore.html): Production Firebase Integration Every Flutter Dev Must Know
- [Caching, Pagination, Lazy Loading & List Performance](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/28-caching-pagination-lazy-loading-list-performance.html): Building Lists That Scale to Tens of Thousands of Items
- [Flutter Performance Profiling & DevTools](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/29-flutter-performance-profiling-devtools.html): Finding and Fixing Performance Problems Systematically
- [Rendering, Jank, Impeller & Memory Optimization](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/30-rendering-jank-impeller-memory-optimization.html): Understanding Flutter's Rendering Pipeline at the Depth Interviewers Expect
- [Testing Strategy: Unit vs Widget vs Integration](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/31-testing-strategy-unit-vs-widget-vs-integration.html): Building a testing philosophy from scratch — cost, coverage, and confidence
- [Unit Testing Dart: Classes, Use Cases & Repositories](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/32-unit-testing-dart-classes-use-cases-repositories.html): Writing meaningful unit tests with mocks — testing what matters without hitting the network
- [Testing BLoC/Cubit: Async State Sequences](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/33-testing-bloccubit-async-state-sequences.html): Verifying state transitions, error paths, and event-to-state mapping with bloc_test
- [Widget Testing: UI Flows, Navigation & Forms](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/34-widget-testing-ui-flows-navigation-forms.html): Testing Flutter UI in a simulated environment — finders, pumps, and interaction simulation
- [Integration Testing & E2E Flows](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/35-integration-testing-e2e-flows.html): Testing complete user journeys on real devices — the final layer of confidence
- [Testability Patterns: Mocks, Fakes, Golden Tests & Coverage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/36-testability-patterns-mocks-fakes-golden-tests-coverage.html): Writing code that is easy to test — the design and tooling decisions that separate senior engineers
- [CI/CD Basics: Build Pipelines, Artifacts & Secrets](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/37-cicd-basics-build-pipelines-artifacts-secrets.html): Automating builds, tests, and deployments — from push to production without manual steps
- [Flavors, Signing, Release Builds & Store Workflows](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/38-flavors-signing-release-builds-store-workflows.html): Managing dev/staging/prod environments, Android signing, and app store submission pipelines
- [Platform Channels: Method Channels, Event Channels & Pigeon](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/39-platform-channels-method-channels-event-channels-pigeon.html): Bridging Flutter and native Android/iOS — calling platform APIs and streaming native data
- [FFI & Native Library Binding](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/40-ffi-native-library-binding.html): Calling C/C++ libraries directly from Dart — maximum performance when platform channels are not enough
- [Push Notifications, Background Work & Deep Links](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/41-push-notifications-background-work-deep-links.html): FCM, local notifications, WorkManager, deep links, and app lifecycle
- [Android Fundamentals for Flutter Engineers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/42-android-fundamentals-for-flutter-engineers.html): Activity lifecycle, Intents, Services, BroadcastReceivers — why Flutter devs must know this
- [Kotlin Coroutines & Flow vs Dart Streams](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/43-kotlin-coroutines-flow-vs-dart-streams.html): Structured concurrency, suspend functions, Flow, StateFlow — and how they map to Dart
- [Jetpack Compose vs Flutter — Tradeoffs & Interview Answers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/44-jetpack-compose-vs-flutter-tradeoffs-interview-answers.html): Declarative UI comparison, state management, navigation, and when to choose each
- [Mobile Security: JWT, Secure Storage, Biometrics & Encryption](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/45-mobile-security-jwt-secure-storage-biometrics-encryption.html): JWT flow, flutter_secure_storage, biometric auth, certificate pinning, OWASP mobile top 10
- [Accessibility: Semantics, Screen Readers & Focus](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/46-accessibility-semantics-screen-readers-focus.html): Semantics widget, TalkBack/VoiceOver testing, focus order, WCAG compliance in Flutter
- [Internationalization & Localization](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/47-internationalization-localization.html): flutter_localizations, ARB files, intl package, RTL, plurals, dynamic locale switching
- [Adaptive UI: Phone, Tablet, Foldables & Desktop](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/48-adaptive-ui-phone-tablet-foldables-desktop.html): LayoutBuilder, MediaQuery, adaptive breakpoints, master-detail, WindowSizeClass, foldables
- [Flutter Web: Rendering, Constraints & SEO Limitations](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/49-flutter-web-rendering-constraints-seo-limitations.html): HTML vs CanvasKit vs Wasm rendering, SEO challenges, when Flutter Web is appropriate
- [Flutter Web + Wasm: What, Why & Deployment](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/50-flutter-web-wasm-what-why-deployment.html): WebAssembly compilation, performance gains, current limitations, deployment workflow
- [Desktop: macOS, Windows, Linux — Packaging & Inputs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/51-desktop-macos-windows-linux-packaging-inputs.html): Shipping Flutter beyond mobile — menus, shortcuts, and native packaging
- [GraphQL vs REST: When to Choose Which](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/52-graphql-vs-rest-when-to-choose-which.html): Understanding query languages, pagination strategies, and caching tradeoffs for senior interviews
- [Firebase vs Supabase vs Custom Backend — Practical Comparison](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/53-firebase-vs-supabase-vs-custom-backend-practical-comparison.html): Choosing the right backend for your Flutter app — tradeoffs, pricing, and migration paths
- [Monetization: IAP, Subscriptions, Ads & RevenueCat](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/54-monetization-iap-subscriptions-ads-revenuecat.html): Implementing in-app purchases, subscription lifecycles, and ad monetization the right way
- [Logging, Observability, Analytics & Feature Flags](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/55-logging-observability-analytics-feature-flags.html): Monitoring production health, understanding user behaviour, and shipping safely with flags
- [System Design I: Real-time Collaboration App](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/56-system-design-i-real-time-collaboration-app.html): Designing a Tixio-style multi-workspace chat platform — architecture, WebSockets, offline queue, and scaling
- [System Design II: Fintech Claims/Payment App](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/57-system-design-ii-fintech-claimspayment-app.html): Designing a secure, compliant payment and claims processing system — auth, encryption, document handling, and error cascades
- [System Design III: Offline-First Field App at Scale](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/58-system-design-iii-offline-first-field-app-at-scale.html): Designing for 10K+ field workers in low-connectivity — sync queues, conflict resolution, and battery optimisation
- [Production Debugging, Incident Response & Root Cause Analysis](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/59-production-debugging-incident-response-root-cause-analysis.html): Triaging crashes, reading stack traces, memory leaks, rollback strategies, and post-mortems
- [CS Basics: Big-O, Memory, Concurrency & Networking](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/60-cs-basics-big-o-memory-concurrency-networking.html): Surviving senior interview CS theory questions — complexity, memory models, and HTTP fundamentals
- [DSA I: Arrays, Strings, HashMaps, Sets & Two Pointers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/61-dsa-i-arrays-strings-hashmaps-sets-two-pointers.html): Pattern recognition for array and string problems — brute force to optimized, with Dart implementations
- [DSA II: Stack, Queue, Linked List, Binary Search & Heap](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/62-dsa-ii-stack-queue-linked-list-binary-search-heap.html): Linear data structures and search algorithms — patterns for undo systems, BFS, and top-K problems
- [DSA III: Trees, Graphs, BFS/DFS, Recursion & Intervals](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/63-dsa-iii-trees-graphs-bfsdfs-recursion-intervals.html): The hardest interview patterns — tree traversal, graph search, recursion thinking, and interval merging
- [Behavioral Foundations: W-STAR, Confidence & Concise Answers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/64-behavioral-foundations-w-star-confidence-concise-answers.html): The framework that makes every behavioral question answerable — with real project stories baked in
- [Project Storytelling: Architecture Decisions, Tradeoffs & Impact](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/65-project-storytelling-architecture-decisions-tradeoffs-impact.html): Turning production experience into compelling interview narratives — architecture, tradeoffs, failure, and quantified impact
- [Leadership & Seniority: Mentoring, Conflict, Deadlines & Product Thinking](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/66-leadership-seniority-mentoring-conflict-deadlines-product.html): Demonstrating senior-level behaviors — leading without a title, navigating disagreement, and thinking beyond code
- [Salary Negotiation, Remote Culture & Relocation](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/67-salary-negotiation-remote-culture-relocation.html): Getting paid what you're worth — research, tactics, remote expectations, and international relocation reality
- [Full Interview Simulation Pack: Technical + Architecture + Behavioral](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/68-full-interview-simulation-pack-technical-architecture.html): Complete mock interview sets with scoring rubrics — the capstone that makes you interview-ready
- [Riverpod 2.x & Provider — The Other State Management](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/69-riverpod-2x-provider-the-other-state-management.html): Master Riverpod's compile-safe, testable state management — and know when to choose it over BLoC
- [Code Generation: freezed, json_serializable & build_runner](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/70-code-generation-freezed-json_serializable-build_runner.html): Automate your model layer — immutable classes, JSON serialization, and unions with zero boilerplate
- [Animations Deep Dive: Implicit, Explicit, Hero & CustomPainter](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/71-animations-deep-dive-implicit-explicit-hero-custompainter.html): From simple fades to complex physics-based motion — master every animation layer in Flutter
- [Memory Profiling, Leak Detection & DevTools Mastery](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/72-memory-profiling-leak-detection-devtools-mastery.html): Find and fix memory leaks, decode the GC, and use DevTools like a performance surgeon
- [Flutter Internals: How Rendering Actually Works](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/73-flutter-internals-how-rendering-actually-works.html): Master the Three Trees, Layout Protocol & Rendering Pipeline
- [gRPC, Protobuf & Advanced API Patterns](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/74-grpc-protobuf-advanced-api-patterns.html): High-Performance Communication Beyond REST
- [KMP vs Flutter — Strategic Comparison for Senior Engineers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/75-kmp-vs-flutter-strategic-comparison-for-senior-engineers.html): Choose the Right Cross-Platform Strategy for Your Team
- [Package Development, Publishing & Open Source Maintenance](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/76-package-development-publishing-open-source-maintenance.html): Build, Publish & Maintain Packages Like a Pro
- [API Integration Without AI: Proving Your Fundamentals in Interviews](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/flutter-interview-mastery/77-api-integration-without-ai-proving-your-fundamentals-in.html): The LinkedIn Post That Exposed 4-5 Year 'Senior' Devs Who Can't Call a REST API Without ChatGPT

## Hard to Replace by AI: Stay Valuable in the Age of Automation

- Course page: https://learn.teamzlab.com/c/hard-to-replace-by-ai.html
- Level: All Levels — No Technical Background Needed
- Lesson count: 18
- About: Free interactive course with 18 lessons for non-tech professionals worried about AI job displacement. Learn the 7 irreplaceable skills AI cannot automate, build workplace visibility, and create a…

### Lessons

- [The Job That Disappeared Overnight](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/01-the-job-that-disappeared-overnight.html): Why Being Good at Your Job Is No Longer Enough
- [What AI Can Actually Do (And What It Can't)](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/02-what-ai-can-actually-do-and-what-it-cant.html): Strategic Knowledge for Your Career
- [The Jobs Most at Risk (And the Ones That Are Safe)](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/03-the-jobs-most-at-risk-and-the-ones-that-are-safe.html): Where Does Your Career Fall on the Spectrum?
- [Emotional Intelligence -- The Skill No Algorithm Has](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/04-emotional-intelligence-the-skill-no-algorithm-has.html): Your Most Valuable Career Asset in the Age of AI
- [Creative Problem-Solving -- Think Like a Human](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/05-creative-problem-solving-think-like-a-human.html): The Ability AI Cannot Replicate
- [Communication That Connects](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/06-communication-that-connects.html): Writing, Speaking, and Persuading in the AI Age
- [Adaptability — The Meta-Skill of 2026](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/07-adaptability-the-meta-skill-of-2026.html): Why the ability to learn anything beats the ability to know everything — and how to build your adaptation muscle
- [Relationship Capital — Your Network Is Your Safety Net](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/08-relationship-capital-your-network-is-your-safety-net.html): Why who knows you matters more than what you know — and how to build genuine relationship capital before you need it
- [Ethical Judgment — The Decisions Only Humans Should Make](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/09-ethical-judgment-the-decisions-only-humans-should-make.html): Why navigating moral gray areas is the ultimate AI-proof skill — and how to build your ethical reasoning muscle
- [Physical Presence — The Untouchable Advantage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/10-physical-presence-the-untouchable-advantage.html): Why jobs that require a human body in a human space are the hardest to automate — and how to leverage presence strategically
- [Stop Being the Best-Kept Secret](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/11-stop-being-the-best-kept-secret.html): Why doing great work is not enough if nobody knows about it — and how to build strategic visibility without being annoying
- [Become the Go-To Person for Something](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/12-become-the-go-to-person-for-something.html): Why the expert in one thing beats the generalist good at everything — and how to choose and build your specialization
- [Learn to Speak AI (Even If You Are Not Technical)](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/13-learn-to-speak-ai-even-if-you-are-not-technical.html): Why AI literacy is the new professional baseline — and how to get fluent without writing a single line of code
- [The 30-Day Career Audit](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/14-the-30-day-career-audit.html): A week-by-week action plan to assess your vulnerability, discover your strengths, and start building your irreplaceability
- [Your AI Toolkit — Tools Every Non-Tech Worker Should Know](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/15-your-ai-toolkit-tools-every-non-tech-worker-should-know.html): A practical guide to the AI tools that make you more productive, more valuable, and harder to replace
- [The Side Hustle Insurance Policy](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/16-the-side-hustle-insurance-policy.html): Why income diversification is your best protection against AI disruption — and how to build it from scratch
- [When It Happens — What to Do If You Lose Your Job to AI](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/17-when-it-happens-what-to-do-if-you-lose-your-job-to-ai.html): A survival playbook for the first 48 hours, the first month, and the strategic pivot that follows
- [You Are Not a Machine — And That Is Your Greatest Advantage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/hard-to-replace-by-ai/18-you-are-not-a-machine-and-that-is-your-greatest-advantage.html): Why becoming MORE human — not more efficient — is the winning career strategy in the age of AI

- Level: Basic to Advanced
- Lesson count: 58
- About: Free interactive IELTS preparation course with 58 lessons. Master all four skills — Reading, Writing, Listening, and Speaking — from basic English grammar to Band 8+ strategies. Covers Academic &…

### Lessons

- [Parts of Speech: Nouns, Verbs, Adjectives & Adverbs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/01-parts-of-speech-nouns-verbs-adjectives-adverbs.html): The Four Pillars of Every English Sentence
- [Sentence Structure: Simple to Compound-Complex](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/02-sentence-structure-simple-to-compound-complex.html): Building Blocks of Clear, Sophisticated Writing
- [Tenses Mastery: Past, Present, Future & Perfect](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/03-tenses-mastery-past-present-future-perfect.html): Controlling Time in English Like a Pro
- [Articles, Prepositions & Connectors](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/04-articles-prepositions-connectors.html): The Small Words That Make a Big Difference
- [Vocabulary Building: The Academic Word List](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/05-vocabulary-building-the-academic-word-list.html): 570 Power Words That Unlock Band 7+
- [Punctuation & Common Writing Errors](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/06-punctuation-common-writing-errors.html): The Invisible Rules That Examiners Always Notice
- [Subject-Verb Agreement & Common Grammar Mistakes](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/07-subject-verb-agreement-common-grammar-mistakes.html): The Rules That Separate Band 6 from Band 7
- [Register & Tone: Formal, Semi-Formal & Informal English](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/08-register-tone-formal-semi-formal-informal-english.html): Speaking the Right Language for the Right Situation
- [IELTS Exam Overview: Format, Scoring & Strategy](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/09-ielts-exam-overview-format-scoring-strategy.html): Know the Battlefield Before You Enter It
- [Band Descriptors Decoded: What Examiners Really Want](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/10-band-descriptors-decoded-what-examiners-really-want.html): Reverse-Engineering the IELTS Scoring System
- [Reading: Skimming & Scanning Techniques](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/11-reading-skimming-scanning-techniques.html): Master the two essential speed-reading skills that unlock every IELTS Reading question type
- [Reading: True/False/Not Given & Yes/No/Not Given](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/12-reading-truefalsenot-given-yesnonot-given.html): Conquer the most confusing IELTS Reading question type with a clear logical framework
- [Reading: Matching Headings & Matching Information](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/13-reading-matching-headings-matching-information.html): Learn to identify main ideas and locate specific details across paragraphs
- [Reading: Multiple Choice & Summary Completion](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/14-reading-multiple-choice-summary-completion.html): Develop precision reading skills for two of the most detail-oriented IELTS question types
- [Reading: Sentence Completion & Short Answer](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/15-reading-sentence-completion-short-answer.html): Perfect the art of extracting precise information from the passage within strict word limits
- [Reading: Diagram & Flow Chart Labeling](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/16-reading-diagram-flow-chart-labeling.html): Master the visual question types that combine reading comprehension with spatial understanding
- [Reading: Speed Reading & Time Management](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/17-reading-speed-reading-time-management.html): Beat the clock — learn to complete 40 questions across 3 passages in exactly 60 minutes
- [Reading: Band 7+ Strategies for Difficult Texts](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/18-reading-band-7-strategies-for-difficult-texts.html): Advanced techniques to handle complex academic passages, dense vocabulary, and tricky question patterns
- [Writing Task 1: Line, Bar & Pie Charts (Academic)](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/19-writing-task-1-line-bar-pie-charts-academic.html): Learn to describe visual data clearly and accurately in 150+ words for IELTS Academic Writing Task 1
- [Writing Task 1: Tables, Maps & Process Diagrams](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/20-writing-task-1-tables-maps-process-diagrams.html): Master the three less common Task 1 visual types that many candidates find most challenging
- [Writing Task 1: Comparing & Contrasting Data](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/21-writing-task-1-comparing-contrasting-data.html): Mastering the Art of Data Comparison
- [Writing Task 1 GT: Letter Writing (Formal, Semi-Formal, Informal)](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/22-writing-task-1-gt-letter-writing-formal-semi-formal-informal.html): The Three Registers of GT Letter Writing
- [Writing Task 1: Band 7+ Language & Structure](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/23-writing-task-1-band-7-language-structure.html): Elevating Your Task 1 to Top-Band Quality
- [Writing Task 2: Essay Types & Planning](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/24-writing-task-2-essay-types-planning.html): Opinion, Discussion, Problem-Solution & Two-Part Questions
- [Writing Task 2: Introduction & Thesis Writing](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/25-writing-task-2-introduction-thesis-writing.html): The First Impression That Sets Your Band Score
- [Writing Task 2: Body Paragraphs — Structure & Evidence](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/26-writing-task-2-body-paragraphs-structure-evidence.html): Building Arguments That Convince Examiners
- [Writing Task 2: Conclusion Mastery](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/27-writing-task-2-conclusion-mastery.html): Ending Your Essay With Impact and Precision
- [Writing Task 2: Coherence, Cohesion & Linking Words](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/28-writing-task-2-coherence-cohesion-linking-words.html): Making Your Essay Flow Like Professional Writing
- [Writing Task 2: Band 7+ Vocabulary & Grammar](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/29-writing-task-2-band-7-vocabulary-grammar.html): The Language Toolkit for Top-Band Essays
- [Listening: Section 1 & 2 Strategies](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/30-listening-section-1-2-strategies.html): Social and Everyday Listening — Your First Points
- [Listening: Section 3 & 4 Strategies](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/31-listening-section-3-4-strategies.html): Academic Discussions & Lectures
- [Listening: Form, Note & Table Completion](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/32-listening-form-note-table-completion.html): Filling in the Gaps Accurately
- [Listening: Multiple Choice & Matching](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/33-listening-multiple-choice-matching.html): Eliminating Distractors Like a Pro
- [Listening: Map & Diagram Labeling](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/34-listening-map-diagram-labeling.html): Navigating Spaces with Your Ears
- [Listening: Numbers, Spelling & Common Traps](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/35-listening-numbers-spelling-common-traps.html): The Details That Make or Break Your Score
- [Listening: Band 7+ Strategies](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/36-listening-band-7-strategies.html): Speed, Accents & Practice Plan
- [Speaking Part 1: Introduction & Familiar Topics](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/37-speaking-part-1-introduction-familiar-topics.html): Making a Strong First Impression
- [Speaking Part 2: The Cue Card](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/38-speaking-part-2-the-cue-card.html): Delivering a 2-Minute Long Turn
- [Speaking Part 3: Discussion & Abstract Topics](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/39-speaking-part-3-discussion-abstract-topics.html): Thinking Deeper and Speaking Smarter
- [Speaking: Fluency & Coherence Techniques](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/40-speaking-fluency-coherence-techniques.html): Flowing Like a River, Not Stumbling Like a Rock
- [Speaking: Vocabulary Range & Collocations](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/41-speaking-vocabulary-range-collocations.html): Sound Natural, Score Higher
- [Speaking: Grammar Range & Accuracy in Speech](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/42-speaking-grammar-range-accuracy-in-speech.html): Complex Structures That Sound Natural
- [Speaking: Pronunciation & Intonation](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/43-speaking-pronunciation-intonation.html): Be Understood, Be Impressive
- [Speaking: Band 7+ — Confidence, Examples & Opinions](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/44-speaking-band-7-confidence-examples-opinions.html): Speak Like a Band 7+ Candidate
- [Advanced Grammar: Conditionals, Passives, Inversions & Cleft Sentences](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/45-advanced-grammar-conditionals-passives-inversions-cleft.html): The Grammar That Separates Band 7 from Band 8
- [Advanced Vocabulary: Collocations, Idioms & Less Common Words for Band 8+](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/46-advanced-vocabulary-collocations-idioms-less-common-words.html): The Lexical Range That Impresses Examiners
- [Paraphrasing Mastery: The #1 IELTS Skill](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/47-paraphrasing-mastery-the-1-ielts-skill.html): Say the Same Thing in a Different Way
- [Critical Thinking: Analyzing & Building Arguments](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/48-critical-thinking-analyzing-building-arguments.html): Think Like a Band 8 Candidate
- [Common Mistakes That Kill Your Band Score](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/49-common-mistakes-that-kill-your-band-score.html): The Errors Examiners See Every Day
- [Academic Writing Style: Hedging, Nominalisation & Formality](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/50-academic-writing-style-hedging-nominalisation-formality.html): Write Like a Scholar, Score Like a Band 8
- [Study Plan: 30-Day, 60-Day & 90-Day IELTS Preparation](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/51-study-plan-30-day-60-day-90-day-ielts-preparation.html): Your Roadmap to Band Success
- [Reading Mock: Full Timed Practice & Review Strategy](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/52-reading-mock-full-timed-practice-review-strategy.html): Simulate Real Reading Test Conditions
- [Writing Mock: Task 1 & Task 2 Self-Assessment](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/53-writing-mock-task-1-task-2-self-assessment.html): Grade Your Own Writing Like an Examiner
- [Listening Mock: Full Test Simulation & Review](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/54-listening-mock-full-test-simulation-review.html): Train Your Ears Under Real Pressure
- [Speaking Mock: Record, Review & Improve Method](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/55-speaking-mock-record-review-improve-method.html): Become Your Own Speaking Examiner
- [Targeting Weak Areas: Plateau Breaking & Focused Practice](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/56-targeting-weak-areas-plateau-breaking-focused-practice.html): When Progress Stalls, Strategy Prevails
- [Test Day Mastery: Timing, Stress Management & Checklist](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/57-test-day-mastery-timing-stress-management-checklist.html): Arrive Prepared, Stay Calm, Perform Your Best
- [Post-Test: Remarking, Retake Strategy & Next Steps](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/ielts-mastery/58-post-test-remarking-retake-strategy-next-steps.html): What to Do After You Get Your Score

## Non-Coding IT Jobs Bootcamp

- Course page: https://learn.teamzlab.com/c/it-jobs-bootcamp.html
- Level: Beginner to Intermediate
- Lesson count: 60
- About: Free non-coding IT career course: 60 lessons covering IT support, helpdesk, system administrator, network, SOC analyst, cloud, M365, Entra, Azure, AWS, backup/DR, ITSM, bank IT officer and corporate…

### Lessons

- [The Non-Developer IT Map](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/01-the-non-developer-it-map.html): Where the real jobs, money, and confusion live
- [How IT Hiring Really Works](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/02-how-it-hiring-really-works.html): Why strong candidates still get filtered out
- [Mindset, Professionalism & Ethics](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/03-mindset-professionalism-ethics.html): Why trust matters more than noise in enterprise IT
- [Hardware Essentials](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/04-hardware-essentials.html): What can fail before Windows even starts
- [Boot Process & Firmware](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/05-boot-process-firmware.html): From power button to login screen
- [File Systems & Storage Troubleshooting](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/06-file-systems-storage-troubleshooting.html): Why disks fail in visible patterns
- [Windows Internals for Support](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/07-windows-internals-for-support.html): The minimum internals every junior must know
- [macOS & Mixed-Endpoint Awareness](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/08-macos-mixed-endpoint-awareness.html): Enough Apple literacy to not freeze in an interview
- [Accounts, Profiles & Permissions](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/09-accounts-profiles-permissions.html): Who the user is changes everything
- [Networking Tools on Windows](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/10-networking-tools-on-windows.html): Fast triage from the command line
- [Office / M365 User Issues](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/11-office-m365-user-issues.html): The everyday pain recruiters expect you to know
- [Imaging, Onboarding & Offboarding](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/12-imaging-onboarding-offboarding.html): The lifecycle work every IT team must get right
- [Remote Support & Ticket Hygiene](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/13-remote-support-ticket-hygiene.html): Support quality is part technical, part process
- [What Active Directory Actually Does](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/14-what-active-directory-actually-does.html): Why AD is the backbone of most enterprises
- [DNS, DHCP & Why AD Breaks When They Break](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/15-dns-dhcp-why-ad-breaks-when-they-break.html): The hidden dependencies behind logon failures
- [OUs, Groups & Group Policy](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/16-ous-groups-group-policy.html): Central control without chaos
- [Share vs NTFS & Access Troubleshooting](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/17-share-vs-ntfs-access-troubleshooting.html): Why ‘I have no access’ is never one-layer simple
- [Patching, Replication & Time Sync](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/18-patching-replication-time-sync.html): Invisible operational hygiene of a domain
- [Domain Joins & Login Failures](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/19-domain-joins-login-failures.html): Safe first actions under identity pressure
- [OSI & TCP/IP Without Confusion](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/20-osi-tcpip-without-confusion.html): How to think in layers under pressure
- [IP Addressing & Subnetting](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/21-ip-addressing-subnetting.html): The math that filters network candidates
- [Switching, VLANs & Trunks](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/22-switching-vlans-trunks.html): One cable, many logical networks
- [Routing, NAT & ACLs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/23-routing-nat-acls.html): How traffic crosses boundaries
- [DNS Deep Dive](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/24-dns-deep-dive.html): The service that breaks everything indirectly
- [VPN, Wi-Fi & Branch Connectivity](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/25-vpn-wi-fi-branch-connectivity.html): Remote access and edge reality
- [Packet Capture & Troubleshooting Flow](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/26-packet-capture-troubleshooting-flow.html): See the truth instead of guessing
- [Linux Filesystem, Users & Permissions](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/27-linux-filesystem-users-permissions.html): Enough Linux to be useful on day one
- [Linux Processes, Services & Logs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/28-linux-processes-services-logs.html): Where Linux incidents become visible
- [Packages, Cron & Routine Automation](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/29-packages-cron-routine-automation.html): Ops work is repetitive by design
- [SSH, Hardening & Remote Access](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/30-ssh-hardening-remote-access.html): Remote admin without being reckless
- [Basic Shell Scripting for Operators](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/31-basic-shell-scripting-for-operators.html): Small scripts, big leverage
- [CIA Triad, Risk & Least Privilege](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/32-cia-triad-risk-least-privilege.html): Security language you must speak clearly
- [Identity Security: MFA, SSO, IAM](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/33-identity-security-mfa-sso-iam.html): Why identity is the new perimeter
- [Phishing, Malware & Ransomware — First 10 Minutes](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/34-phishing-malware-ransomware-first-10-minutes.html): What the first 10 minutes must look like
- [SIEM, Alerts & SOC Triage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/35-siem-alerts-soc-triage.html): How a junior analyst adds value
- [Patching, Vulnerability Mgmt & Baselines](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/36-patching-vulnerability-mgmt-baselines.html): Prevention is boring but hireable
- [Audit-Ready Security: Policies, Logs, Evidence](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/37-audit-ready-security-policies-logs-evidence.html): Controls, logs, and evidence — how regulated IT actually operates
- [Cloud Fundamentals & Shared Responsibility](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/38-cloud-fundamentals-shared-responsibility.html): What moves to cloud and what still stays on you
- [Azure Basics for Support Engineers](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/39-azure-basics-for-support-engineers.html): A practical first Azure map
- [Microsoft 365 & Entra Basics](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/40-microsoft-365-entra-basics.html): The modern identity and productivity stack
- [Intune & Endpoint Management Awareness](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/41-intune-endpoint-management-awareness.html): Managing devices at scale
- [AWS Basics for Non-Developer IT](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/42-aws-basics-for-non-developer-it.html): Enough AWS to survive real interviews
- [Virtualization 101](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/43-virtualization-101.html): One host, many systems
- [VMware, Hyper-V & Proxmox Awareness](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/44-vmware-hyper-v-proxmox-awareness.html): Know the major platforms without overselling
- [Backup Theory That Survives Audits](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/45-backup-theory-that-survives-audits.html): From copy to recoverability
- [RPO, RTO & DR Drills](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/46-rpo-rto-dr-drills.html): Business language for technical people
- [From Tabletop to Real Recovery](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/47-from-tabletop-to-real-recovery.html): How juniors contribute during outages
- [SQL for Operators](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/48-sql-for-operators.html): Just enough database skill to support apps
- [Database Services, Users & Backups](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/49-database-services-users-backups.html): Operational DBA-lite tasks
- [Application Dependency Mapping](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/50-application-dependency-mapping.html): Apps fail in layers, not magic
- [Monitoring & Alert Quality](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/51-monitoring-alert-quality.html): Know what to watch, not just what to install
- [Month-End & Business-Critical Support](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/52-month-end-business-critical-support.html): Why timing changes severity
- [Incident, Problem, Change & Release](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/53-incident-problem-change-release.html): The process spine of enterprise IT
- [Ticket Quality, SLA & Shift Handover](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/54-ticket-quality-sla-shift-handover.html): What operations teams judge every day
- [Banking IT Systems & Payment Rails](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/55-banking-it-systems-payment-rails.html): Enough domain knowledge to sound credible
- [Branch Outage, ATM Outage & Audit Evidence](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/56-branch-outage-atm-outage-audit-evidence.html): Regulated incidents require controlled response
- [Vendor Access, Privileged Access & Compliance Culture](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/57-vendor-access-privileged-access-compliance-culture.html): Trust, controls, and third parties
- [Answering Technical Questions Without Sounding Robotic](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/58-answering-technical-questions-without-sounding-robotic.html): Structure beats over-talking
- [Behavioral, STAR & Salary Discussion](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/59-behavioral-star-salary-discussion.html): Professionalism in the final round
- [Resume, LinkedIn & 90-Day Job Search Plan](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/it-jobs-bootcamp/60-resume-linkedin-90-day-job-search-plan.html): Turn training into interviews

## Free Full-Stack Playbook

- Course page: https://learn.teamzlab.com/c/js-ts-fun-book.html
- Level: Beginner to Intermediate
- Lesson count: 49
- About: Free interactive, project-based playbook: learn TypeScript, NestJS, and React by building real apps. 49 hands-on lessons, quizzes, code playground — no signup required.

### Lessons

- [JavaScript vs TypeScript](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/01-javascript-vs-typescript.html): Adding Superpowers to JS
- [Developer Tooling](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/02-developer-tooling.html): Work Smarter, Not Harder
- [Variables, Types & Arrays](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/03-variables-types-arrays.html): The Building Blocks of Data
- [Objects & Interfaces](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/04-objects-interfaces.html): Structuring Complex Data
- [Functions & Arrow Functions](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/05-functions-arrow-functions.html): Reusable Recipes of Code
- [Modules & Imports](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/06-modules-imports.html): Organizing Code Like a Pro
- [Union Types & Type Narrowing](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/07-union-types-type-narrowing.html): Being Flexible While Safe
- [Classes & OOP](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/08-classes-oop.html): Organizing Code Into Objects
- [Async/Await & Promises](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/09-asyncawait-promises.html): Handling Work That Takes Time
- [Error Handling](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/10-error-handling.html): Catching Problems Before They Crash
- [React — The UI Wizard](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/11-react-the-ui-wizard.html): Building Interfaces Like LEGO
- [State Management & Data Fetching](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/12-state-management-data-fetching.html): Making Your Frontend Talk to Your Backend
- [The Frontend Tech Stack](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/13-the-frontend-tech-stack.html): Meet Your Toolkit
- [API Design & REST](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/14-api-design-rest.html): Building Roads for Your Data
- [Why NestJS?](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/15-why-nestjs.html): Express Grew Up and Got Organized
- [NestJS Architecture Patterns](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/16-nestjs-architecture-patterns.html): Building Clean, Scalable Apps
- [Validation & DTOs](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/17-validation-dtos.html): Never Trust User Input
- [MongoDB — The Flexible Database](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/18-mongodb-the-flexible-database.html): Store Data Like JSON Files
- [Prisma — Your Database Translator](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/19-prisma-your-database-translator.html): Talk to Your Database in TypeScript
- [Environment Variables & Configuration](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/20-environment-variables-configuration.html): Different Settings for Different Worlds
- [Authentication — JWT & Guards](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/21-authentication-jwt-guards.html): Who Are You? Prove It!
- [Generics & Utility Types](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/22-generics-utility-types.html): Writing Code That Works With Any Type
- [Redis — The Speed Demon](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/23-redis-the-speed-demon.html): Super-Fast Temporary Storage
- [Bull — The Job Queue Manager](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/24-bull-the-job-queue-manager.html): Background Tasks Made Easy
- [Email Services](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/25-email-services.html): Sending Emails From Code
- [Push Notifications with Firebase](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/26-push-notifications-with-firebase.html): Ding! You Have a Message!
- [AWS S3 — File Storage](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/27-aws-s3-file-storage.html): Your Files in the Cloud
- [WebSockets & Real-Time](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/28-websockets-real-time.html): Live Updates Without Refreshing
- [Testing — Trust Your Code](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/29-testing-trust-your-code.html): Break Things Before Users Do
- [Docker — Containers Explained](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/30-docker-containers-explained.html): Ship Your App in a Box
- [Git & Version Control](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/31-git-version-control.html): A Time Machine for Your Code
- [Deployment & DevOps](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/32-deployment-devops.html): From Code to Production
- [What is a Monorepo?](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/33-what-is-a-monorepo.html): One Repo to Rule Them All
- [React vs React Native](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/34-react-vs-react-native.html): Web vs Mobile — Same Brain, Different Body
- [Putting It All Together](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/35-putting-it-all-together.html): The Full Picture
- [Your Learning Path Forward](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/36-your-learning-path-forward.html): What to Build Next
- [What is Programming?](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/37-what-is-programming.html): Your First Step into the Digital World
- [Setup Your Toolkit](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/38-setup-your-toolkit.html): Get Your Coding Workshop Ready
- [How the Internet Works](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/39-how-the-internet-works.html): The Web Explained Simply
- [Build Your First Mini App](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/40-build-your-first-mini-app.html): From Zero to Running Code
- [NestJS Setup & First App](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/41-nestjs-setup-first-app.html): From Zero to Running Server in 5 Minutes
- [NestJS Controllers & Routing](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/42-nestjs-controllers-routing.html): The Front Door of Your API
- [NestJS Services & Dependency Injection](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/43-nestjs-services-dependency-injection.html): The Brain Behind Every Controller
- [NestJS Middleware & Interceptors](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/44-nestjs-middleware-interceptors.html): Processing Requests Before and After
- [NestJS Guards & Custom Decorators](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/45-nestjs-guards-custom-decorators.html): Security Checkpoints for Your API
- [NestJS Exception Filters & Error Handling](https://learn.teamzlab.com/lessons/js-ts-fun-book/46-nestjs-exception-filters-error-handling.html): Catching Errors Like a Pro
